summary: Fiery second set with stellar China>Rider and soaring Comes A Time
blurb: The second set erupts with a seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition and a soaring Feel Like A Stranger → Comes A Time jam. Jerry delivers scorching solos on the ballads while Brent adds soulful keyboards. Highlights include Sugar Magnolia, Not Fade Away and an energetic Alabama Getaway/One More Saturday Night encore.
review:
On May 10, 1980 at the Hartford Civic Center the Dead served up a high‑octane second set that still shines in the archives. Opening with a flawless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue, the band rides a wave of energy into a heartfelt Feel Like A Stranger that blossoms into an inspired Comes A Time. Jerry Garcia’s lead work is described as “lighting it up” and “scorching,” especially on the ballad‑heavy jams, while Brent Mydland’s keyboards add a uniquely sublime texture throughout. Phil Lesh anchors the improvisations with a solid (if occasionally understated) bass foundation, and the dual drumming of Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann drives the pre‑drums jam into one of the night’s high points. The set rolls through Estimated Prophet, He’s Gone, Uncle John’s Band and a tight Not Fade Away before closing the main portion with Sugar Magnolia. An encore that builds from Alabama Getaway to One More Saturday Night caps an evening remembered for its seamless segues, powerful solos, and unrelenting groove.

Among the recordings available, the Miller soundboard (gd1980-05-10.set2.sbd.miller.91938.sbeok.flac16) stands out as the definitive source: crisp, well‑balanced mix with excellent dynamics that captures every nuance of this fire‑filled performance. Whether you’re chasing Jerry’s soaring solos or Brent’s soulful keys, this SBD provides the clearest window into a show that still feels fresh after four decades.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Six out of eight recordings are marked positive with consistent praise for the second‑set flow, China>Rider and Comes A Time jams, and Jerry’s solos; two recordings are mixed but do not detract from overall enthusiasm. This strong, repeated positivity warrants a high sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard quality—crisp, well‑balanced mix with good dynamics that captures the full energy of the performance.

key_highlights: Fiery second set with seamless segues (China Cat Sunflower → Rider, Stranger → Comes A Time)
Jerry’s scorching solos on Comes A Time and China>Rider
Brent Mydland’s soulful keyboard work adding flavor to jam sections
High‑energy encore featuring Alabama Getaway and One More Saturday Night

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers scorching, soaring solos throughout, especially on Comes A Time and the China Cat Sunflower → Rider sequence; ballad work is highlighted as powerful and emotive.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid bass foundation with occasional strong ‘brake’ moments on segues; some listeners note a lackluster tone but overall anchors the high‑energy jams.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work described as uniquely sublime, adding soulful depth to jams and vocal contributions (e.g., Far From Me).
band_performance.Mickey: Drum work praised for driving the pre‑drums improvisation and maintaining high energy through the second set.
band_performance.Billy: Partnered with Mickey to deliver energetic, propulsive drumming that fuels the extended jam sections.
